edges.description: Genome-maintenance defects can impair embryonic growth.
Checkpoint and replication-stress defects can impair neurodevelopment.
Impaired developmental cell proliferation contributes to prenatal growth restriction.
Impaired neural progenitor division contributes to reduced brain growth.
Global developmental proliferation defects contribute to severe postnatal growth restriction.
Embryonic mitotic failure contributes to prenatal growth restriction.
CENPJ-associated developmental mitotic failure contributes to reduced brain growth.
Persistent growth impairment follows disrupted embryonic cell division.

evidence.reference_title: Microcephaly, Short Stature, Intellectual Disability, Speech Absence and Cataract Are Associated with Novel Bi-Allelic Missense Variant in RTTN Gene: A Seckel Syndrome Case Report.
evidence.snippet: Forty cases of Seckel syndrome have been reported to date in the literature due to mutations in the ATR, TRAIP, RBBP8, NSMCE2, NIN, CENPJ, DNA2, CEP152 and CEP63 genes.

nodes.description: A subset of Seckel syndrome genes affect ATR-dependent DNA damage response and replication-stress checkpoint signaling, linking genome-maintenance defects to impaired embryonic growth and neurodevelopment.

evidence.reference_title: Microcephaly, Short Stature, Intellectual Disability, Speech Absence and Cataract Are Associated with Novel Bi-Allelic Missense Variant in RTTN Gene: A Seckel Syndrome Case Report.
evidence.snippet: Forty cases of Seckel syndrome have been reported to date in the literature due to mutations in the ATR, TRAIP, RBBP8, NSMCE2, NIN, CENPJ, DNA2, CEP152 and CEP63 genes.

evidence.reference_title: Disruption of mouse Cenpj, a regulator of centriole biogenesis, phenocopies Seckel syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Notably, however, genomic instability was not the result of defective ATR-dependent DNA damage signaling, as is the case for the majority of genes associated with Seckel syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Disruption of mouse Cenpj, a regulator of centriole biogenesis, phenocopies Seckel syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Disruption of the centromere protein J gene, CENPJ (CPAP, MCPH6, SCKL4), which is a highly conserved and ubiquitiously expressed centrosomal protein, has been associated with primary microcephaly and the microcephalic primordial dwarfism disorder Seckel syndrome.

nodes.description: CENPJ disruption impairs centriole and centrosome number control, causes abnormal mitotic spindles, DNA damage, apoptosis, and mitotic failure in a mouse model that recapitulates Seckel syndrome features.

evidence.reference_title: Disruption of mouse Cenpj, a regulator of centriole biogenesis, phenocopies Seckel syndrome.
evidence.snippet: By generating a hypomorphic allele of Cenpj, we have developed a mouse (Cenpj(tm/tm)) that recapitulates many of the clinical features of Seckel syndrome, including intrauterine dwarfism, microcephaly with memory impairment, ossification defects, and ocular and skeletal abnormalities, thus providing clear confirmation that specific mutations of CENPJ can cause Seckel syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Disruption of mouse Cenpj, a regulator of centriole biogenesis, phenocopies Seckel syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Instead, Cenpj(tm/tm) embryonic fibroblasts exhibited irregular centriole and centrosome numbers and mono- and multipolar spindles, and many were near-tetraploid with numerical and structural chromosomal abnormalities when compared to passage-matched wild-type cells.

evidence.reference_title: Disruption of mouse Cenpj, a regulator of centriole biogenesis, phenocopies Seckel syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Increased cell death due to mitotic failure during embryonic development is likely to contribute to the proportionate dwarfism that is associated with CENPJ-Seckel syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Two novel variants in CEP152 caused Seckel syndrome 5 in a Chinese family.
evidence.snippet: Two novel variants in CEP152, c.1060C>T (p.Arg354*) and c.1414-14A>G, were identified in the proband through trio-WES.

evidence.reference_title: Two novel variants in CEP152 caused Seckel syndrome 5 in a Chinese family.
evidence.snippet: Moreover, aberrant skipping of exon 12 due to the non-canonical splice-site variant was revealed by RT-PCR and Sanger sequencing.

nodes.description: Centrosome and centriole gene defects impair neural progenitor division, linking microtubule-organizing center dysfunction to reduced brain and body size.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic Primary Microcephalies: When Centrosome Dysfunction Dictates Brain and Body Size.
evidence.snippet: Most are caused by biallelic or, more rarely, dominant mutations in one of the likely hundreds of genes encoding PM proteins, i.e., ubiquitous centrosome or microtubule-associated proteins required for the division of neural progenitor cells in the embryonic brain.

evidence.reference_title: Microcephaly, Short Stature, Intellectual Disability, Speech Absence and Cataract Are Associated with Novel Bi-Allelic Missense Variant in RTTN Gene: A Seckel Syndrome Case Report.
evidence.snippet: The RTTN gene encodes centriole biogenesis, replication, symmetry and cohesion, basal body organization and has recently been associated with the appearance of microcephaly syndromes.

nodes.description: Prenatal growth restriction is a defining feature of Seckel syndrome and often persists as postnatal proportionate dwarfism.

evidence.reference_title: Two novel variants in CEP152 caused Seckel syndrome 5 in a Chinese family.
evidence.snippet: Background: Seckel syndrome (SCKL) is a rare autosomal recessive inherited disorder, which is mainly characterized by intrauterine and postnatal growth restrictions, microcephaly, intellectual disability, and a typical "bird-head" facial appearance.

nodes.description: Microcephaly is a cardinal neurodevelopmental feature and can be severe.

evidence.reference_title: Two novel variants in CEP152 caused Seckel syndrome 5 in a Chinese family.
evidence.snippet: Background: Seckel syndrome (SCKL) is a rare autosomal recessive inherited disorder, which is mainly characterized by intrauterine and postnatal growth restrictions, microcephaly, intellectual disability, and a typical "bird-head" facial appearance.

evidence.reference_title: Microcephaly, Short Stature, Intellectual Disability, Speech Absence and Cataract Are Associated with Novel Bi-Allelic Missense Variant in RTTN Gene: A Seckel Syndrome Case Report.
evidence.snippet: We present a consanguineous Pakistani family clinically suspected of Seckel syndrome with severe microcephaly, severe intellectual disability, short stature, absence of speech, pointed nose, narrow face and bilateral cataract in two siblings residing in the suburbs of Islamabad.

evidence.reference_title: Microcephaly, Short Stature, Intellectual Disability, Speech Absence and Cataract Are Associated with Novel Bi-Allelic Missense Variant in RTTN Gene: A Seckel Syndrome Case Report.
evidence.snippet: Whole-exome sequencing discovered NM_173630.4: c.57G > T(pGlu19Asp) missense variant in exon 2 of the RTTN gene that co-segregates in the family.

nodes.description: Severe postnatal growth restriction produces short stature within the microcephalic primordial dwarfism spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Microcephaly, Short Stature, Intellectual Disability, Speech Absence and Cataract Are Associated with Novel Bi-Allelic Missense Variant in RTTN Gene: A Seckel Syndrome Case Report.
evidence.snippet: We present a consanguineous Pakistani family clinically suspected of Seckel syndrome with severe microcephaly, severe intellectual disability, short stature, absence of speech, pointed nose, narrow face and bilateral cataract in two siblings residing in the suburbs of Islamabad.
